mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation expansion, so negative month-over-month changes in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns do not occur more often after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or if the sign of the effect flips for more than one 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates compress and the market re-prices a future policy response; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend rises above its prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising UNRATE months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns at least as often as falling or unchanged UNRATE months across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals stronger nominal spending and payroll income, implying improving near-term revenue momentum that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and PAYEMS are above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint above-average condition does not improve 5-day forward SPX hit rate relative to unconditional baseline, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO relative to prior months signals stronger industrial output and inventory replenishment, which supports cyclical earnings and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if months with INDPRO gains above 0.5% do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than lower-growth months, or if the effect is negative over any sustained 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior high level signals housing-demand cooling and weaker future consumption spillovers, which should weigh on equities over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls for 2 consecutive monthly releases after being above 1500
failure_condition: Reject if post-peak HOUST declines are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is indistinguishable from random in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, stable unemployment and expanding payrolls indicate labor-market healing that supports consumer demand and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is flat or lower over 3 months while PAYEMS increases over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor recovery condition fails to improve 60-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if rising payrolls without stable unemployment produce the same predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above the recent neutral band compresses equity multiples and raises recession risk, so the signal becomes negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated versus its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation episodes do not correspond to lower 20-day SPX returns than other regimes, or if the sign remains positive during tightening cycles.
